text: We performed a 4 cm utility incision on a single thoracoscopic access.
There were steady adhesions to the diaphragm.
After dividing them we proceeded with the pulmonary ligament division.
Now we look for the inferior pulmonary vein.
We demonstrate it and stop here for the moment.
The artery is deeply situated in the region where the oblique and the horizontal fissures meet The lower lobe artery originates from the interlobar artery
The basal segmental arteries may have a short common trunk from which two branches originate distal to the superior segmental one, which is preserved.
We divide it by using an endostabler.
The branches to the lower basal segment lies directly behind the transected artery.
We encircle it, clamp it and inflate the lung to ensure that the supra-segmental branch is not compromised.
We return to the pain region.
Expose what it seems to be the basal segmental veins and encycle them.
Before dividing them we return to the branches and divide it ensuring again that the superior segment is not compromised.
With the branches transected it is easier to mobilize the lobe and then we can identify the real superior segmental vein.
which is the uppermost tributary of the inferior pulmonary vein and the three or four basal segmental branches.
It is very important to preserve the superior segmental vein when dividing the basal segmental branches.
The last step is the diffusion of the fissure.
We identify the theoretical fissure and divide it from anterior to posterior by using endo-staplers.
The specimen is removed in the bag.
